title: Whole Blood
description: Blood specimen containing all cellular and plasma components collected directly from the patient.

title: Serum
description: Liquid portion of blood obtained after coagulation, with clotting factors removed.

title: Plasma
description: Liquid component of anticoagulated blood containing clotting factors and dissolved proteins

title: Blood Venous
description: Blood specimen collected from a vein via venipuncture for laboratory analysis.

title: Sputum
description: Respiratory specimen consisting of mucus expectorated from the lower airways for laboratory analysis.

title: Urine
description: Urine sample collected from a patient for biochemical, microscopic and microbiological examination
collection_display: Urine specimen collection, clean catch, mid stream urine

title: Oral Swab
description: Specimen collected using a sterile swab from a oral cavity for microbiological or molecular testing.

title: CSF
description: Cerebrospinal fluid specimen for testing of central nervous system infections, inflammation, hemorrhage and malignancies

title: Stool
description: Fecal specimen collected for microbiological, parasitological, or biochemical testing

title: Ascitic Fluid
description: Peritoneal fluid collected from the abdominal cavity for diagnostic laboratory evaluation.

title: Synovial Fluid
description: Joint fluid aspirated from a synovial cavity for analysis of joint disorders

title: Pleural Fluid
description: Fluid collected from the pleural cavity for laboratory analysis of cells, proteins, microorganisms and biochemical markers to evaluate pleural diseases

title: Nasal Swab
description: Specimen collected from the nasal cavity using a sterile swab for respiratory pathogen testing

title: Throat Swab
description: Specimen collected from the oropharynx using a sterile swab to detect bacterial or viral infections of the throat and upper respiratory tract

title: Bone marrow
description: Bone marrow is a soft, spongy tissue found inside bones, collected as a specimen to evaluate blood cell production and diagnose hematological conditions.

title: Pus
description: Pus is a thick, yellowish or greenish fluid collected from infected tissue, used as a specimen to identify causative organisms and diagnose infections.

title: Skin
description: Skin is the outer protective layer of the body, collected as a specimen (scraping/biopsy) to diagnose infections, dermatological conditions, or other abnormalities.

title: Other
description: Other specimens include any biological materials not classified under standard categories, collected for diagnostic, screening, or research purposes.
